mechanism_narrative: Higher 3-month growth in retail sales signals stronger near-term household demand and upward earnings revisions for consumer-exposed firms, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:RSAFS
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 382928.0 asof t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth does not predict higher 20-day S&P 500 returns, with Rank IC below 0.03 in more than half of 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0% and continues rising, discount-rate pressure dominates earnings growth and lowers equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% and its 3-month change is positive
evidence_refs: series:FEDFUNDS
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 4.24 asof t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations have positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or their mean return is not below unconditional mean return.

mechanism_narrative: A rising unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, causing investors to cut earnings expectations and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: series:UNRATE
fact:UNRATE latest snapshot value 5.0 asof t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if 60-day S&P 500 returns following the trigger are not lower than unconditional returns or if the triggered hit rate for negative returns is below 55%.

mechanism_narrative: Positive momentum in industrial production reflects improving real output and operating leverage, raising near-term earnings expectations and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 113.9019 asof t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if positive INDPRO 3-month momentum is followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in 50% or fewer observations, or if Rank IC is negative over a 3-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Joint strength in payrolls and retail sales confirms that income growth is converting into spending, supporting broad revenue growth and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change is positive and RSAFS 3-month percent change is positive
evidence_refs: series:PAYEMS
series:RSAFS
fact:PAYEMS latest snapshot value 138495.0 asof t-1mo
fact:RSAFS latest snapshot value 382928.0 asof t-1mo
failure_condition: Reject if the joint PAYEMS-RSAFS signal has no incremental 20-day return prediction beyond either series alone, or if its mean triggered return is below baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating consumer prices combined with a flat federal funds rate signals falling real policy restraint and inflation risk, pressuring margins and valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 6-month annualized growth exceeds its 12-month growth and FEDFUNDS 3-month change is less than or equal to 0
evidence_refs: series:CPIAUCSL
series:FEDFUNDS
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 210.565 asof t-2mo
fact:FEDFUNDS latest snapshot value 4.24 asof t-1mo
regime_hint: inflation scare with insufficient policy tightening
failure_condition: Reject if triggered 60-day S&P 500 returns are positive in more than 60% of cases or if the interaction term adds no explanatory power versus CPIAUCSL alone.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp decline in housing starts signals weakening construction activity and household balance-sheet stress, reducing future consumption and earnings expectations, producing negative S&P 500 returns over 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST falls more than 10% from its level 12 months earlier
evidence_refs: series:HOUST
fact:HOUST latest snapshot value 1187.0 asof t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the HOUST decline trigger is not followed by below-median 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations.

mechanism_narrative: Falling industrial production alongside rising consumer prices indicates stagflationary pressure, lowering real growth while raising cost pressure, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO 3-month percent change is negative and CPIAUCSL 3-month percent change is positive
evidence_refs: series:INDPRO
series:CPIAUCSL
fact:INDPRO latest snapshot value 113.9019 asof t-2mo
fact:CPIAUCSL latest snapshot value 210.565 asof t-2mo
failure_condition: Reject if the combined INDPRO-CPIAUCSL trigger produces positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or has no negative interaction coefficient in walk-forward tests.
